# MentalHappyAlternatives

Exploring alternatives to MentalHappy? Try Whereby for frictionless, in-browser video rooms; join curated group sessions on Herd; find niche coaches and 1:1 guidance on Truly; or get ADHD-focused accountability with Shimmer ADHD. Each offers a distinct path to community and support.

results.title: Sidebar Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
#### What's great

curated groups (1)technology support (1)bespoke meeting solution (1)expert facilitation (1)

Report

•1 review

I've been lucky to be working with people in Sidebar for a bit now and it's been amazingly helpful. Having a set of people who have similar goals and challenges, to talk with, that you trust, and can get help from makes a huge difference. As people get better at their craft and more senior in their careers, it becomes harder and harder to have a great, independent set of people to learn from and bounce ideas off of, Sidebar delivers on this. And the caliber of people is impressive and energizing.

#### What's great

professional network (3)community building (4)high caliber peers (4)

Report

•1 review [...] side, our facilitator was phenomenal! In the other group I had joined we were each "taught" to facilitate and took turns throughout the year. That had very mixed results and Sidebar's dedicated group facilitators was actually a key reason I was willing to try this concept again. Chase did not disappoint - he's amazing! The Sidebar team clearly has a good process on the human side of things too. I was also impressed that once I was matched to a group a special space opened up for us where we could clearly see the milestones for the entire journey ahead and link to materials already made available to us. There were also all of the standard features whereby I could see the group members and our group chat, open new chats with 1 or more members, control notifications, etc. Overall we're off [...] Caveat that this review is at the very beginning of my journey with Sidebar - I have literally only completed the first group meeting as of 02 June 2023. However, I joined a different group designed for women in late 2020 that I was a little disappointed with. I can already see HUGE advantages in Sidebar's approach, particularly with the tech supports. Most exciting to me was the meeting experience! They are using a bespoke solution inside of their community and at the top it has this outline of the meeting agenda with planned time for each activity. In real time a little gauge is moving across to show everyone where we are now and how much time is left for that activity. Like, WOW! I want this for ALL of my meetings! On the human side, our facilitator was phenomenal! In the other group I

I wasn't content creator. I work in sales and I’m building a brand for first-time dads. Nas.io helped me go from idea to a real paid product in under a month. The platform is fast, intuitive, and the community behind it is even better. Their team? Responsive, generous, and on it. Also Intercom support has been 10/10 every time. I signed up as a Platinum user, no regrets at all!!! If you’re trying to build a real community with real revenue, Nas.io actually gets you there.

user-friendly (5)community building (8)monetization options (3)

•1 review [...] 4.9

Based on 15 reviews

Review Nas.com?

Reviewers describe Nas.io as an easy, low-friction way to launch digital products and run communities without juggling multiple tools. Users repeatedly praise simple setup, landing pages, email tools, events, and especially WhatsApp integration, saying it helps members stay in a familiar space while improving access control and discussion quality. Several also credit AI features and ads tools with helping them move from idea to paid offers or leads quickly. Support is another common theme: reviewers say the team is responsive, hands-on, and genuinely helpful.
